chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Along with this we also have something called Keywords in Python

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Keywords are basically reserved words

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Reserved words means these are the words which have a fixed meaning in Python

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: These words can never be used as a variable or function

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: For example we have false

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Now in Python, fixed means that false is a boolean value and false means it will always be false

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: It's not like we will make a variable and assign a value to it

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: This is not possible

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Similarly we have none, true, lambda, else

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: There are many such keywords which we will learn as we go through the Python chapters

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Similarly there are some new keywords in every chapter which we will learn

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So we don't have to remember these or make notes of them

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: We just need to have a general understanding that there are some reserved words in Python

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: which can't be used as variables

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: We have read false, none, true for now

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: We will read many of these words in this lecture in detail

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Along with this there is something called comments in Python

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So let's clear this

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Basically whenever we want to write something in our code

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: which we want to tell to some other programmer or developer

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: or to some other AI engineer

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So these things are not a part of our code

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Generally we write these things in English

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So we write these things in the form of comments

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Like people pass comments in real life

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Similarly in program, if we want to write something in English

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Not in Python, we have to write something in English

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So we write it in the form of comments

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: To write comments, we simply have to start the comment with hash

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Generally all our code editors highlight the comments with green colors

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So whatever we write in hash, our Python interpreter will ignore it

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: This is something which is only meant for humans, not for machines

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Like here I can write any false value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Like I wrote false is equal to 1, 2, 3

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: This is something which we know that if we write in Python syntax

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: If we write false is equal to 1, 2, 3, then we will get an error

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Like if I run this code, I am going to get this error

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: False 1, 2, 3 is a syntax error

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Syntax error means it is going against the rules of Python

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: It is breaking the rules of Python

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: It cannot assign to false

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: This 1, 2, 3 value cannot be assigned to false

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Because false cannot be a variable

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So we got an error

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: But if we write the same thing in comments

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Like here we print type of age

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: We have removed this prime

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So this comment, Python has completely ignored it

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So we can write anything in comments

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Generally we tell in comments that what our code, program or function performs

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Like for example, here on the top I can write a comment

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Our first program

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So this is basically English

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: As such, Python is not being used in this

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: And interpreter will ignore this comment

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Now like we have single line comments with the help of hash

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Similarly we have multiple line comments

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Means if we want we can write anything in multiple lines

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: To write a multi-line comment

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: We have to use triple quotes

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: And if we write anything in triple quotes

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: This is a multi-line comment

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So this is also a thing which our code will ignore

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: Let's save it and run it

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So in that case, this is going to get ignored

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: So these are our multi-line comments

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: And these are our single line comments
